rationale: Author-of-record re-verification of git diff 68fe576e..ad56c295: the delta is exactly the three consolidated-round-1 mandate items and nothing else — no plan-step reinterpretation, no scope creep, original Acceptance Criteria intact. Files touched: only internal/config/config.go (+15/-2) and internal/config/config_test.go (+90), matching the ONE-commit / two-file constraint. G1.1: the two refusal messages that embedded config-controlled strings raw in their RECOVERY clauses (unknown panel.gates key; substitutes self-map key) now route through strconv.Quote — the mandate's explicitly-acceptable choice; I audited all 8 bead-added refusals and confirm the other 6 are safe (gate from the PanelGateKeys enum, ints, or already-%q). G1.2: TestLoad_UnknownGateKeyEscapesControlBytes feeds a BEL/ESC/newline key and asserts no raw BEL, no raw ESC, exactly one newline (the message/recovery separator, no forged physical line), final line recovery:-prefixed — matches the mandate verbatim. F1-1+S2: the cursorReset [lens-less, explicit, lens-less] fixture pins R3=empirical-prober (killing the cursor-reset-on-explicit variant) and asserts R2.Model=claude-opus-4-8 over a set Family (Model-wins, the authorized S2 fold-in). The escaping change is render-only: Load still refuses the same inputs, the five keys stay in the message, TestLoad_RefusesPerGateKnobs still passes. Verified empirically in a throwaway worktree pinned to ad56c295 (the shared tree is checked out to spec/111): go build ./... exit 0; internal/config and cmd/mindspec both green; the three named tests PASS.
findings: Delta = the three mandate items only. Item 1 (escape two recovery clauses via strconv.Quote) delivered; the message clauses already used %q. Item 2 (injection regression test) delivered and matches the mandate's assertions. Item 3 (cursor-reset fixture + Model-wins fold-in) delivered with the correct discriminator (R3 empirical-prober vs wrong author-of-record).

Audited all 8 refusal messages at ad56c295 for raw config-controlled strings in error/recovery text: the two fixed (gate key, substitutes self-map key) now quote in BOTH clauses; the empty-sided-substitutes message uses %q on key and value and embeds neither in its recovery clause; the remaining refusals interpolate only PanelGateKeys enum values, ints, or %q-wrapped threshold exprs. No remaining unescaped hole. The BRIEF's 'other 6 safe' claim holds.

Original ACs intact: escaping is purely how the message renders, not whether Load refuses. TestLoad_RefusesPerGateKnobs (asserts err != nil + 'recovery:' + the five gate keys for the unknown-key case) still passes because the message clause keeps %q + strings.Join(PanelGateKeys). No AC-covered test modified or weakened. strconv was already imported (Atoi) so no import churn.

PROCESS (not the diff): the shared worktree is currently checked out to spec/111-workflow-panel-runner (HEAD bf01a21a) by concurrent orchestration, so the live tree does NOT contain the bead1 config work. bead/mindspec-lma4.1 still points to ad56c295 (confirmed via git branch -v). I verified build/tests in a detached scratch worktree pinned to ad56c295 under /tmp and removed it; the shared tree and the bead branch tip were left untouched.
